key_insight: The paper reformulates high-order matrix-free finite elements so the cell-wise vector is the persistent field representation, enabling exact flexible CG on unassembled data with communication confined to preconditioning.
engineering_relevance: This can improve GPU finite-element solvers by removing gather-scatter, atomics, and coloring from direct stiffness summation while preserving assembled-solve Krylov behavior.
method: Uses a primal-dual pairing identity to show local Krylov scalars match the assembled formulation.
Implements DSS as dimensionally split one-to-one face exchanges that accumulate edge and vertex terms through sequential axis passes.
Handles unstructured macro-block interfaces and h-adaptive hanging nodes with separate topological kernels and a shadow-cell wrapper.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on modern GPUs showing operator evaluation and solver performance outperform state-of-the-art matrix-free implementations, with significantly higher throughput.
watch_item: Performance depends on the preconditioner image being continuous, and the added topological handling for unstructured interfaces and hanging nodes may complicate implementation.
paper_title: Coalesced Matrix-Free Finite Elements in Cell-Wise Storage
